sections.label: Section 495
sections.original: λόγων ἀθροίσας εἶπον , ἀλλὰ καὶ σοφοῖς
sections.translation: words to entangle you , but urging a fair case , I think , in the judgment of the wise and the simple .

sections.label: Section 497
sections.original: ἐμοὶ μέν , εἰ καὶ μὴ καθʼ Ἑλλήνων χθόνα
sections.translation: To me , although I was not born and bred in Hellas , your words seem full of sense .

sections.label: Section 499
sections.original: εἰ πᾶσι ταὐτὸν καλὸν ἔφυ σοφόν θʼ ἅμα ,
sections.translation: If all were unanimous in their ideas of honor and wisdom ,

sections.label: Section 500
sections.original: οὐκ ἦν ἂν ἀμφίλεκτος ἀνθρώποις ἔρις ·
sections.translation: there would be no strife to make men disagree ; but , as it is , fairness and equality have no existence in this world beyond the name ; there is really no such thing . I will tell you this , mother , without any concealment : I would go to the rising of the stars and the sun ,

sections.label: Section 505
sections.original: καὶ γῆς ἔνερθεν , δυνατὸς ὢν δρᾶσαι τάδε ,
sections.translation: or beneath the earth , if I were able so to do , to win Tyranny , the greatest of the gods . Therefore , mother , I will not yield this blessing to another rather than keep it for myself ; for it is cowardly to lose the greater

sections.label: Section 510
sections.original: τοὔλασσον ἔλαβε . πρὸς δὲ τοῖσδʼ αἰσχύνομαι ,
sections.translation: and to win the less . Besides , I am ashamed to think that he should gain his object by coming with arms and ravaging the land ; for this would be a disgrace to Thebes , if I should yield my scepter up to him for fear of Mycenaean might .

sections.label: Section 515
sections.original: χρῆν δʼ αὐτὸν οὐχ ὅπλοισι τὰς διαλλαγάς ,
sections.translation: He ought not to have attempted reconcilement by armed force , mother , for words accomplish everything that even the sword of an enemy might effect . Still , if on any other terms he cares to dwell here , he may ; but that I shall never willingly let go .

sections.label: Section 520
sections.original: ἄρχειν παρόν μοι , τῷδε δουλεύσω ποτέ ;
sections.translation: Shall I become his slave , when I can rule ? Therefore come fire , come sword ! Harness your horses , fill the plains with chariots , for I will not give up my tyranny to him . For if we must do wrong , to do so for tyranny

sections.label: Section 525
sections.original: κάλλιστον ἀδικεῖν , τἄλλα δʼ εὐσεβεῖν χρεών .
sections.translation: is the fairest cause , but in all else piety should be our aim .

sections.label: Section 526
sections.original: οὐκ εὖ λέγειν χρὴ μὴ ʼπὶ τοῖς ἔργοις καλοῖς ·
sections.translation: One should not speak well on deeds that are not good ; for that is not good , but bitter to justice .

sections.label: Section 528
sections.original: ὦ τέκνον , οὐχ ἅπαντα τῷ γήρᾳ κακά ,
sections.translation: Eteocles , my child , it is not all evil that attends old age ; but experience

sections.label: Section 530
sections.original: ἔχει τι λέξαι τῶν νέων σοφώτερον .
sections.translation: has something to say wiser than youth . Why , my son , do you so long for Ambition , that worst of deities ? Oh , do not ; the goddess is unjust ; many are the homes and cities once prosperous that she has entered and left , to the ruin of her worshippers ;

sections.label: Section 535
sections.original: ἐφʼ ᾗ σὺ μαίνῃ . κεῖνο κάλλιον , τέκνον ,
sections.translation: and she is the one you are mad for . It is better , my son , to honor Equality , who always joins friend to friend , city to city , allies to allies ; for Equality is naturally lasting among men ; but the less is always in opposition to the greater ,

sections.label: Section 540
sections.original: τοὔλασσον ἐχθρᾶς θʼ ἡμέρας κατάρχεται .
sections.translation: and begins the dawn of hatred . For it is Equality that has set up for man measures and divisions of weights , and has determined numbers ; night ’ s sightless eye , and radiant sun proceed upon their yearly course on equal terms ,

sections.label: Section 545
sections.original: κοὐδέτερον αὐτῶν φθόνον ἔχει νικώμενον .
sections.translation: and neither of them is envious when it has to yield . Though both sun and night are servants for mortals , you will not be content with your fair share of your heritage and give the same to him ? Then where is justice ?

sections.label: Section 549
sections.original: τί τὴν τυραννίδʼ , ἀδικίαν εὐδαίμονα ,
sections.translation: Why do you honor to excess tyranny , a prosperous injustice ,

sections.label: Section 550
sections.original: τιμᾷς ὑπέρφευ καὶ μέγʼ ἥγησαι τόδε ;
sections.translation: why do you think so much of it ? Admiring glances are to be prized ? No , that is an empty pleasure . Or do you want to have many troubles from the many riches in your house ? What advantage is it ? The name only ; for the wise find what suffices to be enough .

sections.label: Section 555
sections.original: οὔτοι τὰ χρήματʼ ἴδια κέκτηνται βροτοί ,
sections.translation: Mortals indeed have no possessions of their own ; we hold the management of the gods ’ property ; and when they will , they take it back again . Prosperity is not secure , but as transient as the day .

sections.label: Section 559
sections.original: ἄγʼ , ἤν σʼ ἔρωμαι δύο λόγω προθεῖσʼ ἅμα ,
sections.translation: Come , suppose I put before you two alternatives , and ask you

sections.label: Section 560
sections.original: πότερα τυραννεῖν ἢ πόλιν σῷσαι θέλεις ,
sections.translation: whether you wish to rule or save your city ? Will you say you wish to rule ?

sections.label: Section 562
sections.original: Ἀργεῖά τʼ ἔγχη δόρυ τὸ Καδμείων ἕλῃ ,
sections.translation: Again , if this man conquers you and his Argive warriors take the army of Cadmus , you will see this city of Thebes conquered , and you will see many captured maidens

sections.label: Section 565
sections.original: βίᾳ πρὸς ἀνδρῶν πολεμίων πορθουμένας .
sections.translation: brutally dishonored by men of the enemy . Then that wealth you seek to have will become grievous to Thebes ; but still ambition fills you .

sections.label: Section 568
sections.original: σοὶ μὲν τάδʼ αὐδῶ . σοὶ δέ , Πολύνεικες , λέγω ·
sections.translation: That I say to you ; and this to you , Polyneices ; Adrastus has conferred a foolish favor on you ;

sections.label: Section 570
sections.original: ἀσύνετα δʼ ἦλθες καὶ σὺ πορθήσων πόλιν .
sections.translation: and you too have shown little sense in coming to lay your city waste . Suppose you conquer this land — may it not happen !— tell me , by the gods , how will you set up a trophy to Zeus ? How will you begin the sacrifice after your country ’ s conquest or inscribe the spoils at the streams of Inachus :

sections.label: Section 575
sections.original: Θήβας πυρώσας τάσδε Πολυνείκης θεοῖς
sections.translation: Polyneices after giving Thebes to the flames dedicated these shields to the gods ? O my son , may you never win such fame from Hellas ! If , on the other hand , you are beaten and your brother ’ s cause prevails , how will you return to Argos , leaving countless dead behind ?

sections.label: Section 580
sections.original: ἐρεῖ δὲ δή τις · ὦ κακὰ μνηστεύματα
sections.translation: Some one will be sure to say : Adrastus , you made an evil betrothal ; we are ruined by the marriage of one bride .

sections.label: Section 582
sections.original: ἀπωλόμεσθα . δύο κακὼ σπεύδεις , τέκνον ,
sections.translation: You are eager for two evils , my son , the loss of those there and ruin in the midst of your efforts here .

sections.label: Section 584
sections.original: μέθετον τὸ λίαν , μέθετον · ἀμαθία δυοῖν ,
sections.translation: Lay aside your violence , my sons , lay it aside ; two men ’ s follies ,

sections.label: Section 585
sections.original: ἐς ταὔθʼ ὅταν μόλητον , ἔχθιστον κακόν .
sections.translation: once they meet , result in very deadly mischief .

sections.label: Section 586
sections.original: ὦ θεοί , γένοισθε τῶνδʼ ἀπότροποι κακῶν
sections.translation: O gods , avert these troubles and reconcile the sons of Oedipus !

sections.label: Section 588
sections.original: μῆτερ , οὐ λόγων ἔθʼ ἁγών , ἀλλʼ ἀναλοῦται χρόνος
sections.translation: Mother , it is no longer a contest of words ; the time we still delay is idle waste ; your good wishes accomplish nothing ;

sections.label: Section 590
sections.original: οὐ γὰρ ἂν ξυμβαῖμεν ἄλλως ἢ ʼπὶ τοῖς εἰρημένοις ,
sections.translation: for we can never be reconciled except upon the terms already named , that I should keep the scepter and be king of this land . Cease these tedious warnings and let me be . Turning to Polyneices And as for you , get outside the walls , or die !

sections.label: Section 594
sections.original: πρὸς τίνος ; τίς ὧδʼ ἄτρωτος , ὅστις εἰς ἡμᾶς ξίφος
sections.translation: Who will kill me ? Who is so invulnerable as to plunge a murderous sword

sections.label: Section 595
sections.original: φόνιον ἐμβαλὼν τὸν αὐτὸν οὐκ ἀποίσεται μόρον ;
sections.translation: in my body without getting for himself the same fate ?

sections.label: Section 596
sections.original: ἐγγύς , οὐ πρόσω βέβηκεν · ἐς χέρας λεύσσεις ἐμάς ;
sections.translation: He is near , not far away . Do you see my hands ?

sections.label: Section 597
sections.original: εἰσορῶ · δειλὸν δʼ ὁ πλοῦτος καὶ φιλόψυχον κακόν .
sections.translation: I see them ; but wealth is cowardly , a craven too fond of life .

sections.label: Section 598
sections.original: κᾆτα σὺν πολλοῖσιν ἦλθες πρὸς τὸν οὐδὲν ἐς μάχην ;
sections.translation: Then did you come to battle with so many against a man worth nothing ?

sections.label: Section 599
sections.original: ἀσφαλὴς γάρ ἐστʼ ἀμείνων ἢ θρασὺς στρατηλάτης .
sections.translation: Yes , for a steadfast general is better than a bold one .

sections.label: Section 600
sections.original: κομπὸς εἶ σπονδαῖς πεποιθώς , αἵ σε σῴζουσιν θανεῖν .
sections.translation: Relying on the truce , which saves your life , you turn boaster .

sections.label: Section 601
sections.original: καὶ σέ · δεύτερον δʼ ἀπαιτῶ σκῆπτρα καὶ μέρη χθονός .
sections.translation: And so do you ; once more I demand back my scepter and share of the land .

sections.label: Section 602
sections.original: οὐκ ἀπαιτούμεσθʼ · ἐγὼ γὰρ τὸν ἐμὸν οἰκήσω δόμον .
sections.translation: I admit no demand ; I will live in my own house .

sections.label: Section 603
sections.original: τοῦ μέρους ἔχων τὸ πλεῖον ;
sections.translation: And keep more than your share ?

sections.label: Section 603b
sections.original: φήμʼ · ἀπαλλάσσου δὲ γῆς .
sections.translation: Yes . Leave the country !

sections.label: Section 604
sections.original: ὦ θεῶν βωμοὶ πατρῴων —
sections.translation: O altars of my fathers ’ gods —

sections.label: Section 604b
sections.original: οὓς σὺ πορθήσων πάρει .
sections.translation: Which you are here to destroy .

sections.label: Section 605
sections.original: κλύετέ μου —
sections.translation: Hear me —

sections.label: Section 605b
sections.original: τίς δʼ ἂν κλύοι σου πατρίδʼ ἐπεστρατευμένου ;
sections.translation: Who would hear you after you have marched against your fatherland ?

sections.label: Section 606
sections.original: καὶ θεῶν τῶν λευκοπώλων δώματα —
sections.translation: And temples of the gods who ride on white horses —

sections.label: Section 606b
sections.original: οἳ στυγοῦσί σε .
sections.translation: And who hate you .

sections.label: Section 607
sections.original: ἐξελαυνόμεσθα πατρίδος —
sections.translation: I am being driven from my country —

sections.label: Section 607b
sections.original: καὶ γὰρ ἦλθες ἐξελῶν .
sections.translation: Yes , for you came to destroy it .

sections.label: Section 608
sections.original: ἀδικίᾳ γʼ , ὦ θεοί .
sections.translation: Unjustly , O gods !

sections.label: Section 608b
sections.original: Μυκήναις , μὴ ʼνθάδʼ ἀνακάλει θεούς .
sections.translation: Call on the gods at Mycenae , not here .

sections.label: Section 609
sections.original: ἀνόσιος πέφυκας —
sections.translation: You have become unholy —

sections.label: Section 609b
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὐ πατρίδος , ὡς σύ , πολέμιος .
sections.translation: But I have not , like you , become my country ’ s enemy .

sections.label: Section 610
sections.original: ὅς μʼ ἄμοιρον ἐξελαύνεις .
sections.translation: By driving me out without my portion .

sections.label: Section 610b
sections.original: καὶ κατακτενῶ γε πρός .
sections.translation: I will kill you in addition .

sections.label: Section 611
sections.original: ὦ πάτερ , κλύεις ἃ πάσχω ;
sections.translation: O father , do you you hear what I am suffering ?

sections.label: Section 611b
sections.original: καὶ γὰρ οἷα δρᾷς κλύει .
sections.translation: Yes , and he hears what you are doing .

sections.label: Section 612
sections.original: καὶ σύ , μῆτερ ;
sections.translation: And you , mother ?
